sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 7.36 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.7)

statement.latex: Is it true that every residually finite group in which every subgroup of finite index (including the group itself) is defined by a single defining relation is either free or isomorphic to the fundamental group of a compact surface? { O.\,V.\,Mel'nikov} No; for example, let$H_n= x,y y^{-1}xy=x^n$,$n=2,3,$; then every subgroup of finite index in$H_n$is isomorphic to a group$H_m$for some$m$(V.\,A.\,C...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
